# CWE-89 positive: concatenated SQL from request parameter (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-89-vulnerable.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
)

// CWE-89 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
// Vulnerable line: fmt.Sprintf builds SQL with user-supplied email.
func FindCustomer(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB) {
	email := r.URL.Query().Get("email")
	query := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT id FROM customers WHERE email = '%s'", email)
	row := db.QueryRow(query)
	var id int
	if err := row.Scan(&id); err != nil {
		http.Error(w, "missing", http.StatusNotFound)
		return
	}
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]int{"id": id})
}
